scenarios.description: setScores accepts an array of power-of-2 length 1 and updates scores and height (branch-false)
scenarios.precondition: Prepare a MiniMaxAlgorithm instance with default scores and height; using real implementation
scenarios.expectedResult: scores array reference equals the provided array of length 1; height equals 0
pseudocode: MiniMaxAlgorithm algo = new MiniMaxAlgorithm(); int[] newScores = new int[]{5};

scenarios.description: setScores accepts an array of power-of-2 length 4 and updates scores and height (branch-false)
scenarios.precondition: MiniMaxAlgorithm instance created normally; no mocks
scenarios.expectedResult: scores array reference equals the provided array of length 4; height equals 2
pseudocode: MiniMaxAlgorithm algo = new MiniMaxAlgorithm(); int[] newScores = new int[]{1,2,3,4};

scenarios.description: setScores rejects an array of non-power-of-2 length 3 without changing state (branch-true)
scenarios.precondition: MiniMaxAlgorithm algo has initial scores and height stored
scenarios.expectedResult: scores remains unchanged (original reference); height remains unchanged; no exception thrown
pseudocode: MiniMaxAlgorithm algo = new MiniMaxAlgorithm(); int[] originalScores = algo.getScores(); int originalHeight = algo.getHeight(); int[] newScores = new int[]{7,8,9};
assert algo.getScores() == originalScores; assert algo.getHeight() == originalHeight;

scenarios.description: setScores throws NullPointerException when passed null
scenarios.precondition: MiniMaxAlgorithm instance created; passing null will invoke parameter access
